<findings>

[observation] [info] Isolated files — no import edges between 5 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0)
[observation] [info] Cohesion is 0 because static web assets have no import relationships — the graph metric is not meaningful here; evaluate zone health structurally rather than by cohesion score.
[relationship] [warning] No build step links static-site-assets content to the Swift source zones — llms.txt and sitemap.xml are manually authored, creating documentation drift risk on every feature release.
[anti-pattern] [warning] sitemap.xml and site.webmanifest are hand-authored with no link to the authoritative version variable in app-build-scripts. Each app release can ship with stale metadata in the static site without any CI signal — the dual-artifact release model requires a shared version source, not two independently maintained version strings.
[suggestion] [warning] Add a CI step that extracts the CFBundleShortVersionString from Info.plist (or the VERSION variable from app-build-scripts) and writes it into sitemap.xml lastmod dates and any versioned download links in docs/ — eliminating the manual sync requirement on each release.
[suggestion] [info] Add a docs/404.html file with site navigation so GitHub Pages serves a branded error page instead of the platform default when a URL is not found.
[suggestion] [info] Zone "Static Site Assets" (static-site-assets) has at-risk risk (score: 0.50, cohesion: 0.00, coupling: 0.00) — approaching architectural risk thresholds

</findings>

<insights>

- Isolated files — no import edges between 5 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0)
- Isolated files — no import edges between 5 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0)
- Add a llms-full.txt alongside llms.txt to give AI consumers richer context about the project's full API and architecture, following the emerging llms.txt convention.
- The site.webmanifest and CNAME together imply a hosted static deployment (likely GitHub Pages) — ensure the CI pipeline validates these files on each release to prevent silent drift.
- robots.txt and sitemap.xml are present but there is no explicit cache or CDN invalidation strategy documented; add a note in the deployment runbook so SEO assets are refreshed on each release.
- Cohesion is 0 because static web assets have no import relationships — the graph metric is not meaningful here; evaluate zone health structurally rather than by cohesion score.
- No build step links static-site-assets content to the Swift source zones — llms.txt and sitemap.xml are manually authored, creating documentation drift risk on every feature release.
- No file in static-site-assets references the app version string defined in app-build-scripts (Makefile, build scripts, or Info.plist). sitemap.xml lastmod dates and site.webmanifest metadata will silently lag the shipping version after every release unless a generation step writes them from the authoritative version source.
- sitemap.xml and site.webmanifest are hand-authored with no link to the authoritative version variable in app-build-scripts. Each app release can ship with stale metadata in the static site without any CI signal — the dual-artifact release model requires a shared version source, not two independently maintained version strings.
- The docs/ directory has no 404.html file; GitHub Pages custom domains serve the platform default error page when one is absent, breaking site branding and navigation for any mistyped or expired URL.
- If any HTML or Markdown file in docs/ contains a hardcoded download URL pointing to a versioned DMG artifact, that URL becomes stale on every app release and must be updated manually — a release-time sed substitution or template variable driven from the authoritative version source in app-build-scripts would make this automatic.
- llms.txt describes the project for AI consumers but has no programmatic link to the GoToBedCore public API surface (schedule types, validation rules, store interface) — AI assistants consuming only llms.txt will produce suggestions inconsistent with actual domain types unless the file is regenerated from source on each release.
- Add a docs/404.html file with site navigation so GitHub Pages serves a branded error page instead of the platform default when a URL is not found.
- Add a CI step that extracts the CFBundleShortVersionString from Info.plist (or the VERSION variable from app-build-scripts) and writes it into sitemap.xml lastmod dates and any versioned download links in docs/ — eliminating the manual sync requirement on each release.

</insights>
